description_long: This object is a rectangular, upright telephone booth with a deep blue exterior reminiscent of the iconic British police box style. The booth features a flat roof with a slightly raised section, and the upper portion is adorned with a series of small, square, light-colored window panes on all visible sides. The front side displays a distinct white panel, likely representing a notice or instruction sign, and the rest of the booth is covered in dark blue panels with subtle geometric detailing. The windows are arranged in two rows, with the upper row being more prominent and the lower row more subtle. There is no visible door handle, but the booth appears to have a door on the front side, as indicated by the paneling and window arrangement. The overall design suggests a sturdy, enclosed structure suitable for outdoor urban environments.
description: A dark blue, rectangular telephone booth with small square windows and a white notice panel on the front, styled like a classic British police box. The booth is fully enclosed with geometric paneling and a flat roof.
description_view_0: The booth is seen from the back, showing two rows of small square windows and no signage or white panel.
description_view_1: This side view displays the booth's window arrangement and blue paneling, with no signage or white panel visible.
description_view_2: The front view reveals the white notice panel and the main door, along with the characteristic window arrangement.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing only the window arrangement and blue paneling.
attribute_window_configuration: two rows of small square windows on all four sides
attribute_signage_or_labeling: white rectangular notice panel on the front door, no other visible signage
